title: Cozy Japanese Sugar Dumplings
description: A traditional Japanese dish featuring sweet and fluffy sugar dumplings that are perfect for a cozy night in. This recipe is low-sodium-friendly and can be served as a snack or dessert.

ingredients.name: Granny Smith apple, peeled and grated
1/4 teaspoon salt
ingredients.notes: use low-sodium salt for a low-sodium-friendly option

instructions: In a large bowl, combine caster sugar, all-purpose flour, and salt. Mix well until combined.
Add the grated apple, egg, and cinnamon (if using) to the dry ingredients. Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces. Shape each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat. Brush with a small amount of vegetable oil.
Cook the dumplings for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy on the outside.
Remove the dumplings from the pan and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the dumplings warm with a dusting of matcha powder (if using).
